name: Upham
coordsSource: web-verified:getthedata-centre-Upham(50.98142,-1.23822);getthedata SO32-1JE Widlers Lane(50.981750,-1.238796 ~50m) at Upham village/church/school; stored 50.9672,-1.211 & centroid 50.96637 sit ~2km SE at Lower Upham (separate settlement) rejected; 2026-06-04
overview: Upham is a village and parish on the edge of the South Downs National Park in Hampshire, lying between Winchester and Bishop's Waltham, about 10 miles from central Southampton. The parish has a population of around 620 across roughly 254 houses and two centres: the original hilltop hamlet of Upham (a conservation area with the church, recreation ground, village pond and Brushmakers Arms) and Lower Upham to the south-west (with the village hall and the Alma Inn). It is a lively community with a primary school, shop, church, two pubs and even its own brewery.
character: A charming, active downland village in two parts, with strong community life, country pubs and an extensive footpath network through surrounding farmland.
amenities: Two pubs: the Brushmakers Arms (Upham) and the Alma Inn (Lower Upham)
Two village halls / recreation grounds and a village pond
Active societies and a successful football club
Bus 69 (Winchester Road) and S5 shopper service

schools.name: Secondary schools in the Bishop's Waltham/Swanmore area

prices.summary: A sought-after South Downs village; period and detached homes in a conservation-area setting, with limited supply supporting values. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walking the Monarch's Way, Pilgrims' Trail and King's Way footpaths
Upham Brewery and the village pubs
Church of the Blessed Mary and conservation-area lanes
Village events: Safari Supper, Church Fete, drama and cycling clubs
pros: Two characterful pubs plus a village brewery
Primary school, shop and church retained
On the edge of the South Downs National Park with great walking
cons: Spread across two centres (Upham and Lower Upham)
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a lively, characterful South Downs-edge village with pubs, a brewery and good walking, within reach of Winchester.
primarySupermarket: Village shop; supermarkets in Bishop's Waltham/Hedge End

sources.label: Blessed Mary, Upham - A Church Near You
